name: NordicTrack T 6.5Si Treadmill
whatWorks: The 3.0 CHP motor handles sustained jogging and running reliably — owners report it running without hesitation at 6–8 MPH for 45-minute sessions, which is the real test of home treadmill motor quality.

iFIT integration opens a library of thousands of on-demand and live workouts — Google Maps runs, studio classes, and auto-adjusting incline programs — that meaningfully extend the machine's usefulness beyond a basic belt and motor.
300 lb weight capacity is honest and consistent with reported use, making this appropriate for a wider range of users than budget treadmills that list 250 lb but flex noticeably under load.

The 12% incline range adds enough variety for incline walking workouts, which are among the most effective low-impact cardio formats — owners who do '12-3-30' style workouts consistently report it as their primary use case.
worthKnowing: The iFIT subscription runs ~$39/month (or ~$396/year) — over three years of ownership this adds more to the total cost than the machine itself. Without a subscription, the display shows only basic speed and time data.

The 7" display is noticeably small, especially compared to Peloton Tread and iFIT-branded competitors with 14–22" screens. Buyers who want an immersive display experience should budget for a separate tablet mount.

Assembly is complex and involves 25+ steps and 40+ bolts. NordicTrack's own instructions assume two people; solo assembly is reported as 3+ hours. Professional assembly ($150–200 CAD) is worth considering.
Motor noise increases meaningfully above 6 MPH. In apartments or homes with shared floors, this is a real limitation for daytime and evening use.

commonComplaints.title: iFIT subscription required for full value…
commonComplaints.body: The machine works without iFIT but it's basically just a belt and motor. The whole reason to buy this over a cheaper treadmill is the iFIT classes, and that's another $400 a year.

commonComplaints.title: 7-inch screen feels tiny in use…
commonComplaints.body: I put my iPad next to it because the built-in screen is too small to actually watch anything. At this price I expected something bigger.

commonComplaints.title: Assembly took us almost four hours…
commonComplaints.body: Assembly was brutal. Instructions weren't clear, bolts stripped on one of the arms, and we had to watch YouTube to figure out the last steps. Pay for the assembly service.

commonComplaints.title: Gets loud above 6 MPH…
commonComplaints.body: At walking and light jogging speeds it's fine, but once I hit 7 MPH it starts vibrating and the motor noise picks up noticeably. My downstairs neighbour has already knocked.

verdict: The NordicTrack T 6.5Si is a competent mid-range treadmill whose value proposition is almost entirely tied to iFIT — without the subscription, you're paying $999 for a motor and belt that competitors offer for $600. With iFIT, the on-demand class library and auto-adjusting incline programs justify the price for buyers who will actually use them consistently. The 7" display is the clearest compromise at this price; bring a tablet if screen size matters. Assembly complexity is real and professional installation is worth the cost for most buyers.

realBuyers.quote: The iFIT classes are the reason I actually use this thing. I've done more cardio in the last 3 months than the last 3 years.
I do incline walking at 12% every morning. The motor handles it without a complaint. Great for low-impact calorie burn.
At 285 lbs I was nervous but this thing doesn't flex or wobble at all. Solid machine.
Running on it daily for 8 months with zero issues. Worth every penny if you actually use it.

sources.title: NordicTrack T 6.5Si Review — Garage Gym Reviews

metaDescription: NordicTrack T 6.5Si treadmill review for Canada: 3.0 CHP motor, 12% incline, iFIT compatible. Honest on subscription cost and small display. ~$999 CAD.
introText: The NordicTrack T 6.5Si is a mid-range home treadmill with a 3.0 CHP motor, 12% incline, and iFIT compatibility for on-demand workout classes. It's aimed at buyers who want more than a basic belt but aren't ready to spend $2,000+. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B088X5R4C3) at around $999 CAD.
